title: Cannes Boat Rental: How to Choose the Perfect Vessel for 2026
html:
When planning a boating trip in Cannes, the first step is to decide what kind of experience suits your party and budget, from a shared tour at EUR 80 per person to a crewed luxury yacht at EUR 5,000 per day. Whether departing from Quai Laubeuf or the Port de la Rade, BoatHire24 makes it straightforward to compare verified listings and book a boat online for your 2026 getaway.

Where to set off in Cannes

Cannes offers several departure points depending on your chosen boat and itinerary. The main marinas include Vieux Port (Port de Cannes) at the heart of the Croisette, Port Pierre Canto with larger berths, and the newer Port du Mouré Rouge. For a quick escape to the islands, head to Quai Laubeuf near the Palais des Festivals. Nearby, the Îles de Lérins (Sainte-Marguerite and Saint-Honorat) lie only 15 minutes away, while a longer cruise can reach Golfe-Juan or Antibes. The bay of Cannes itself is sheltered, making it suitable for most boat types. You can book a boat online in a few minutes.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cannes?

Prices for boat rental in Cannes range from EUR 80 per person for a shared tour to over EUR 5,000 per day for a crewed superyacht. The exact cost depends on the size, age, and equipment of the vessel, as well as the season. Here is a breakdown of typical price bands: One option worth a look is this motor yacht.

  • Shared tour or water taxi: from EUR 80 per person for a 2-hour trip
  • Small motorboat (e.g. Axopar 28) half-day: from EUR 450 without fuel
  • Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42) full-day: from EUR 1,800 with skipper
  • Luxury crewed yacht (e.g. Azimut 60) full-day: from EUR 4,000 all inclusive

Which boat to rent

Choosing the right boat means matching your group size and planned activities to the vessel's features. Consider these options: You can also see more at browse the listings.

  1. Motorboat, best for a day trip to the Îles de Lérins, up to 8 guests, from EUR 400 half-day
  2. Rib or tender, ideal for watersports and speed, up to 6 guests, from EUR 350 half-day
  3. Catamaran, perfect for stable cruising and sunbathing, up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,500 full-day
  4. Sailboat, suits sailing enthusiasts exploring the bay, up to 8 guests, from EUR 800 full-day
  5. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Fairline Targa 43), for a premium experience with cabin space, up to 10 guests, from EUR 1,200 half-day, have a look at the Fairline Targa 43 listing for full specifications

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cannes?

In France, a boating licence is required for motor vessels over 6 knots, but many rental operators provide skippered charters so you do not need one yourself. For bareboat rental (without skipper), the operator will confirm exact licence requirements based on the boat's power and dimensions. Always check the listing details. A local tip:

In summer, the mistral wind can pick up from the northwest, so if you are self-driving, always check the afternoon forecast and return before the sea breeze strengthens around 3 pm.
The peak season in Cannes runs from June through August, when prices are highest and availability lowest.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80 to 5,000+ per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: 2 to 12 guests typically
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: Not required when skipper included; check for bareboat

Questions about boat rental in Cannes

Can I rent a boat in Cannes without a licence?

Yes, you can rent a boat with a skipper who holds the necessary qualifications, which covers you for any vessel.

What is the best month to rent a boat in Cannes?

September offers warm weather and calm seas with fewer crowds and lower prices than August.

How far is it from Cannes to Monaco by boat?

The distance is approximately 35 nautical miles, taking about 1.5 hours at cruising speed in a motor yacht.

Can I anchor overnight in the Bay of Cannes?

Yes, but it is restricted in some areas near the islands; check local mooring rules or choose a marina berth.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned mariner, renting a boat in Cannes opens up the stunning coastline and the Lérins Islands. BoatHire24 connects you with trusted owners, so you can browse the listings and find the right vessel for your group. If you own a boat in Cannes, consider listing it on the platform to reach travellers planning their 2026 holidays.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cannes and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
